detailed_analysis: Both scoped files are small, cohesive support modules for the v0.5 mcp_server JSON adapter. _helpers.py (62 lines) is a set of five tiny pure functions for shape coercion and recursive env-reference rewriting; dialect.py (70 lines) is a single frozen dataclass describing per-adapter spellings plus one normalization method. No CRITICAL or MAJOR anti-patterns are present: no secrets, no injection, no silent swallowing, no mutable defaults, no no-op bodies, no God Class, no Long Function (largest is canonical_transport at 7 lines), no deep nesting, no cyclic imports, no global mutable state. Fail-fast is honored well: as_string_list and as_mapping raise descriptive ValueError, and canonical_transport raises on unsupported transports. The two real code-health issues are (1) a DRY duplication between canonicalize_env_refs and render_env_refs, which are byte-for-byte the same recursive walker differing only in where the style string comes from, and (2) primitive-obsession on the env_reference_style and command_mode dataclass fields, which are bare str defaults with an implicit closed value set (canonical/claude/gemini/opencode and split/...) validated only downstream at render time rather than at the dataclass boundary. On spec conformance the code itself is clean, but the mcp_server_io package is absent from architecture.md §4 (module map) and §9 (traceability) even though it carries FR-09 (mcp_server matrix) and participates in the NFR-15 secret-policy egress path; §9 explicitly states a requirement with no carrying module documented is a defect, so this is a governance/traceability deviation.

findings.problem: canonicalize_env_refs (lines 32-39) and render_env_refs (lines 42-49) are structurally identical recursive tree walkers; the only difference is the style argument source (literal "canonical" vs dialect.env_reference_style). canonicalize_env_refs is render_env_refs with style fixed to "canonical". The duplicated traversal must be edited in two places for any change to the recursion shape (e.g. handling tuples or None).

env_reference_style and command_mode are bare str fields whose valid values form a closed enumeration (env_reference_style: canonical/claude/gemini/opencode, enforced in mcp_secret_policy.format_env_reference; command_mode: split/...). The dataclass is frozen but performs no validation of these fields at construction, so an invalid env_reference_style is only rejected later at render time (format_env_reference raises 'unknown env reference style'), deferring the fail-fast boundary away from the configuration object. A Literal/Enum type or a __post_init__ check would validate at the boundary.

The alias lookup dict is rebuilt from the static transport_aliases tuple on every call (line 62). The dataclass is frozen, so transport_aliases never changes after construction; the mapping could be computed once. Correctness is unaffected; this is a minor avoidable allocation. Acceptable given low call frequency, noted for completeness.

deviations.description: The mcp_server_io package (including dialect.py and _helpers.py) does not appear in the architecture.md §4 Module map nor in the §9 traceability table. §9 carries FR-09 (mcp_server matrix) and NFR-15 (secret handling) at the adapter layer, and explicitly states 'Any future requirement that has no carrying module is a defect in either the requirement or the architecture.' The v0.5 mcp_server JSON adapter package is the de-facto carrier of FR-09 yet is undocumented in the layer/module map. Code is correctly placed in Layer 3 (Interface Adapters) and honors the Dependency Rule; the deviation is a traceability/documentation gap, not a layering violation.

verdict_summary: Clean, cohesive Layer-3 support modules; zero CRITICAL/MAJOR findings, one DRY duplication and one primitive-obsession smell, plus an architecture-doc traceability gap for the mcp_server_io package.
